mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods and residential investment, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpaces demand indicators during a monetary tightening cycle, forcing equity markets to price in margin compression and lower forward earnings, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered periods during rate hiking phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above trend correlates with negative 60-day forward equity returns in more than 55% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward market returns.
failure_condition: CPI acceleration above the 0.5pp threshold consistently coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that sustains consumer demand and equity earnings growth over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in housing starts reduces construction activity and related consumer spending, signaling weakening aggregate demand that pressures corporate revenues and drives negative equity returns over a quarterly period.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline by more than 10% over six months while S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of historical instances.
